title: Andrew_Hevesi
raw_text:
Andrew D. Hevesi (born November 19, 1973) is a Democratic member of the New York State Assembly representing the 28th Assembly District, which includes Forest Hills, Rego Park, Richmond Hill, Glendale, Kew Gardens, Ridgewood, and Middle Village.


== Early life and family ==
Hevesi is the son of former New York State Comptroller Alan Hevesi, and the brother of former New York State Senator Daniel Hevesi.
Andrew Hevesi held several public service positions before being elected to the New York State Assembly, including a period in both the Queens District Attorney's office and as Director of Community Affairs for Public Advocate Betsy Gotbaum. Hevesi also served as Chief of Staff for former New York State Senator Jeff Klein.
Hevesi has a BA degree in political science from Queens College. He married Rachel Ross in 2007. The couple has a daughter.


== Political career ==
On May 10, 2005, Hevesi won a special election to fill a seat left vacant by the resignation of former Assemblyman Michael Cohen, serving for the remainder of his term. In 2007, New York State and Albany County investigators reviewed whether Hevesi and his brother had benefited from their father Alan Hevesi's role as State Comptroller. Hevesi was re-elected in November 2006 and has been re-elected in all subsequent elections.
From June 2011 to February 2015 Assemblyman Hevesi served as Chairman of the Committee on Oversight, Analysis, and Investigation, a position previously held by both New York City Comptroller Scott Stringer and New York State Senate President Pro Tempore Jeff Klein. As Chair, Assemblyman Hevesi held hearings on numerous topics including healthcare, human trafficking, and technology infrastructure in New York State.
Hevesi has secured a total of $6 million for freight locomotive engine upgrades in the 2013–2014 and 2014–2015 New York State Budgets.
In February 2015, Assemblyman Hevesi was appointed Chairman of the Committee on Social Services.
Since 2021, Hevesi has served as Chair of the Assembly Committee on Children and Families, where his work has focused on child welfare policy, including foster care, child protective services, and access to child care. During his tenure, legislation has been advanced related to raising the minimum age of arrest, reforming the state’s child protective reporting system, and expanding access to child care and family supports.
In the state budget, Hevesi has led expansions to the Empire State Child Tax Credit, including increased benefit levels and broader eligibility for families. Additional funding has also been allocated for child care workforce support and access to subsidized care.
In his district, Hevesi has continued constituent services and local engagement initiatives. Since 2021, his office has organized a volunteer series consisting of more than 30 community service events, with over 250 participants.


=== Controversy ===
In 2026, Hevesi accused Democratic primary opponent Jonathan Rinaldi of submitting a fraudulent change-of-party affiliation form to the Board of Elections in an attempt to interfere with Hevesi's ballot access.


== Election results ==
May 2005 special election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

November 2006 general election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

November 2008 general election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

November 2010 general election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

November 2012 general election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

September 2012 Democratic primary election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

November 2012 general election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

September 2014 Democratic primary election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

November 2014 general election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

September 2016 Democratic primary election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

November 2016 general election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

September 2018 Democratic primary election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

November 2018 general election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

June 2020 Democratic primary election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

November 2020 general election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

June 2022 Democratic primary election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

November 2022 general election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD

November 2024 general election, NYS Assembly, 28th AD
